headlines.title: Powell will remain on Fed board until DOJ probe is over
headlines.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell made it clear Wednesday he’s not going anywhere just yet.

headlines.title: Iran war has cost the U.S. $25 billion so far, Pentagon official says
headlines.description: A top Pentagon official said the cost of the ongoing U.S. war with Iran is estimated to be $25 billion so far, as the conflict nears a 60-day mark.

headlines.title: Secret Service officer was hit by gunfire from correspondents' dinner shooting suspect, sources say
headlines.description: Investigators believe that the man charged with the attempted assassination of President Donald Trump shot a Secret Service officer who was wearing a ballistic vest, according to three law enforcement officials familiar with the case

headlines.title: Comey’s ‘86 47’ post was ‘a bad idea’ but ‘not a crime,’ says House Democrat
headlines.description: Rep. Jason Crow (D-Colo.) joins Meet the Press NOW to react to the latest indictment of former FBI Director James Comey over an image he posted of seashells spelling out “86 47." The Trump administration previously tried and failed to indict Rep. Crow over a video telling soldiers to disobey illegal orders.

headlines.title: Hegseth calls Democrats the 'biggest adversary’ in the Iran war
headlines.description: NBC News correspondents Christine Romans, Courtney Kube, Monica Alba and Raf Sanchez join Meet the Press NOW as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine testify on Capitol Hill for the first time since the Iran war began.

headlines.title: Jerome Powell calls his time as Fed chair a 'privilege' during last press conference
headlines.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said his time as head of the central bank has been a "privilege," as he gave his last press conference as chair. The Senate Banking Committee advanced President Donald Trump’s nominee to succeed Powell, Kevin Warsh. Powell will remain at the Fed after May 15, when his tenure as its chair ends.

headlines.title: How the White House Correspondents' Association dinner chaos unfolded
headlines.description: Hundreds of journalists, celebrities and politicians, including President Donald Trump and the two men next in line to succeed him, had taken their seats at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner Saturday night when, one floor above, a man rushed toward a Washington Hilton Hotel security checkpoint armed with guns and knives

headlines.title: Trump may send Afghan allies who were promised new lives in the U.S. to Congo instead
headlines.description: Hundreds of Afghan refugees who helped the United States fight the Taliban may be sent to the Democratic Republic of Congo after the Trump administration halted their U.S. resettlement, an advocacy group said

headlines.title: How the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner chaos unfolded
headlines.description: Secret Service faced challenges evacuating President, Vice President, Cabinet officials and Congressional leaders after gunman rushes D.C. gala.

headlines.title: Supreme Court limits use of race in redistricting in a win for GOP
headlines.description: The Supreme Court ruled for Republicans in a congressional redistricting case from Louisiana, ruling that a new map was a racial gerrymander even though it was drawn to comply with the Voting Rights Act.

headlines.title: Senators introduce bipartisan bill banning Chinese vehicles and auto parts
headlines.description: A bipartisan Senate duo introduced a bill on Wednesday to ban the importation of Chinese-made vehicles and auto parts, weeks ahead of U.S.

headlines.title: Florida legislature passes redistricting plan creating four more GOP-leaning House seats
headlines.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis got his maps
